zh.lifeSummary: 當Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr於1328年出生在威爾士蒙姆爾聖 皮埃爾時，他的父親Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor47歲，母親Angharad verch Morgan38歲。他和Nest verch Gwilym Sais至少有4個兒子和1個女兒。他去世於1388年之後在英國英格蘭。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr于1328年出生在威尔士蒙姆爾聖 皮埃爾时，他的父亲Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor47岁，母亲Angharad verch Morgan38岁。他和Nest verch Gwilym Sais至少有4个儿子和1个女儿。他去世于1388年之后在英国英格兰。

nameMeanings.name: ap
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Dutch: occupational name for a cobbler.
Slovenian: nickname for a talkative person, from lapati ‘to babble’, or a topographic name from dialect lap ‘abyss’. Compare Lapp 4.

variants.name: Lapp
meanings: German, English, and Dutch: from Middle High German and Middle English lappe, Middle Dutch lap ‘cloth, patch, rag’, a metonymic occupational name for a mender of clothes or shoes, or a nickname given to someone who wore ragged clothes or garments with loose folds, flaps, or hanging sleeves, or who had fleshy folds of skin on his face, neck, or body.

German: nickname for a simple-minded person, from Middle Low German lappe ‘fool’. This surname (in any of the two possible senses; see also 1 above) is also found in France (mainly Alsace, also Lorraine).
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French lappe ‘burdock’, perhaps for someone with a prickly character.

nameMeanings.name: Llywelyn
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Llewellyn  .

variants.name: Llewellyn
meanings: Welsh: from the personal name Llywelyn, derived from British Celtic Lugubelinos, itself from the divine name Lugus + an uncertain second element often used alone as a name. Through association with llew ‘lion’, it was altered to Llewelyn. This was the name of two princes of north Wales who held the Normans at bay for many years in the 13th century. Etymologically speaking, the form with a fourth -l- is an error, not found as an original Welsh name, but this is now much the most common spelling in the US.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: possibly a metonymic occupational name for a fisherman, from orfe, a species of freshwater fish. Alternatively, a habitational name from Urff near Kassel, formerly Orpha.

variants.name: Orff
meanings: German: variant of   Orf  .

nameMeanings.name: Sain
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of German Zirn, which is a variant of Zürn (see Zurn ). Compare Saine , Sane , and Sayne .

Croatian and Serbian (Šain): metonymic occupational name for a hawker or a nickname from ša(h)in ‘falcon, hawk (trained for hunting)’, a word of Turkish and ultimately Persian origin (see Sahin ). Compare Shain and Shine .

Americanized form of Slovenian and Croatian Šajn: nickname from šajn, an obsolete loanword from German meaning ‘shine’, or a Slavicized form of German Schein . As a Croatian surname it may also be a variant of Šain (see 2 above). Compare Shain and Shine .

variants.name: Sahin
meanings: Turkish (Şahin): from şahin ‘common buzzard’ or ‘hawk’, a word of Persian origin, applied as an ornamental name, nickname, or metonymic occupational name for a hawker (compare Sain 2). This is the fourth most frequent surname in Turkey. Compare Shahin .
Bangladeshi and Indian: variant of   Shahin  .

variants.name: Sane
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Sachin, Shashikant, Suresh, Anand, Ashok, Deepak, Dhiraj, Jayant, Madhu, Purushottam, Sandeep, Shekhar.  African/Muslim Amadou, Demba, Lamin, Mohamadou.
Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.
Indian (Maharashtra; pronounced as two syllables): Konkanastha Brahmin name, apparently a descriptive nickname derived from Marathi sana ‘small’, a word of Kannada origin.

variants.name: Zurn
meanings: German (Zürn): nickname for an irascible man, from Middle High German zurn ‘angry’. Compare Sain .

variants.name: Saine
meanings: Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.
French: probably a variant of Saïn (see Sain 6).
West African (Gambia): variant of the Serer name Sène (see Sene 2).

variants.name: Sayne
meanings: Variant of   Sain   1, a surname of German origin.

nameMeanings.name: Pyr
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (north-West Midlands, Lancashire, Yorkshire, and Norfolk): nickname from Middle English (Old French) pie ‘magpie’, which could be given to someone who wore black and white clothing, had grey-streaked dark hair, or had a loud, chattering voice and impertinent behaviour, or was cunning or sly. The uncomplimentary senses derive from the bird's behavior and are recorded uses of the word in Middle English and early Modern English.

English: occasionally perhaps a topographic or habitational name referring to a house or inn named Pie ‘magpie’. There were two London taverns so named, but whether these gave rise to a hereditary surname is not known. Surnames derived from house and inn signs are rare in English.
English (of Norman origin): nickname from Anglo-Norman French and Middle English pie ‘merciful, compassionate, kind’, a variant of Old French, Anglo-Norman French piu, peu; see Pew (2).

variants.name: Pew
meanings: Welsh: variant of   Pugh  .
English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French pi(u), piw, peu ‘holy, pious’, also ‘merciful, kind, compassionate’.

variants.name: Pyatt
meanings: English (mainly Staffordshire): nickname either from Middle English piot ‘magpie’ (Old French pïot, a diminutive of Old French pie), commonly used to denote a chattering woman, or alternatively from an unrecorded Middle English pyard, an uncomplimentary term derived from the same Old French word + -(h)ard. Compare Pye .
Variant of   Piatt  , a surname of French and Walloon origin.
History: This surname (see 2 above) is listed along with the variant Piatt in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Pie
meanings: English: variant of   Pye  , in part a cognate of 2 below.
French: nickname from Old French pie ‘magpie’, perhaps given to a talkative person or someone who had a loud, chattering voice.
French (Pié): variant of Piet ‘foot’. Compare Pease and Pee .

nameMeanings.name: Philip
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From the Greek name Philippos, meaning ‘lover of horses’, from philein ‘to love’ + hippos ‘horse’. This was popular in the classical period and since. It was the name of the father of Alexander the Great . It was also the name of one of Christ's apostles, of a deacon ordained by the apostles after the death of Christ, and of several other early saints. See also Philippa .

en.lifeSummary: When Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr was born in 1328, in St Pierre, Monmouthshire, Wales, his father, Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor, was 47 and his mother, Angharad verch Morgan, was 38. He had at least 4 sons and 1 daughter with Nest verch Gwilym Sais. He died after 1388, in England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr est né en 1328, à St Pierre, Monmouthshire, Galles, son père, Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor, avait 47 ans et sa mère, Angharad verch Morgan, avait 38 ans. Il a eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille avec Nest verch Gwilym Sais. Il est décédé après 1388, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr 1328 in St Pierre, Monmouthshire, Wales geboren wurde, war sein Vater Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor 47 und seine Mutter Angharad verch Morgan 38. Er hatte mit Nest verch Gwilym Sais mindestens 4 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb nach 1388 in England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr è nato il nel 1328 nel St Pierre, Monmouthshire, Galles, il suo padre, Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor, aveva 47 anni e il suo madre, Angharad verch Morgan, aveva 38 anni. Lui avuto almeno 4 figli e 1 figlia con Nest verch Gwilym Sais. Morì dopo il 1388, nel Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyrが1328年にウェールズモンモウスシュアイアスト ピエアで生まれた時，父Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manorは47歳，母Angharad verch Morganは38歳であった。彼はNest verch Gwilym Saisとの間に少なくとも4人の息子と1人の娘をもうけた。彼は1388年以後にイギリスイギリスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr(이)가 1328년 웨일스 몬모우스실르 스트 피르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor(은)는 47세였고, 그의 어머니 Angharad verch Morgan(은)는 38세였다. 그는 Nest verch Gwilym Sais(와)과 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1388년 이후 영국 잉글랜드에서 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr родился в 1328 г. в местности ст пьирр, монмоутшир, Уэльс, его отцу по имени Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor было 47 лет, а матери, которую звали Angharad verch Morgan, 38. У него было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь от Nest verch Gwilym Sais. Он умер после 1388 г. в местности Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philip ap Llywelyn of Sain Pyr nació en 1328, en St Pierre, Monmouthshire, Gales, su papá, Llywelyn Ap Ifor Lord of St. Clear manor, tenía 47 años y su mamá, Angharad verch Morgan, tenía 38 años. Tuvo por lo menos 4 hijos y 1 hija con Nest verch Gwilym Sais. Murió después de 1388, en Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
